title: [Feature]: Evaluate a Google Cloud Storage sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Google Cloud Storage as a target-only connection. nats-sinks has S3 and OCI Object Storage backlog items but not a Google Cloud Storage-specific sink.
proposal: Research a Google Cloud Storage sink with deterministic object names, conditional object creation for duplicate safety, checksum verification, optional compression compatibility, payload encryption support, and workload identity or service-account authentication.
users: Google Cloud operators, data platform teams, and cross-cloud event archive users.
delivery_semantics: Success may be returned only after the object write or composed object has been durably accepted by Google Cloud Storage. Duplicate redelivery must be controlled through conditional writes or verification.
security: Service-account keys should be avoided where workload identity is possible. Bucket names, object prefixes, metadata, and payloads must be treated as sensitive and redacted from logs.
acceptance: Research identifies object write, precondition, checksum, and retry semantics.
The design supports deterministic object keys and duplicate policies.
Configuration validates bucket, prefix, region/location expectations, and timeouts.
Unit tests use storage fakes and no network calls.
Documentation covers IAM, retention, lifecycle, and cost considerations.
tests: Add unit tests for object-key rendering, conditional write behavior, checksums, duplicate decisions, and error classification. Gate live tests.
documentation: Add docs/google-cloud-storage-sink.md if implemented and update roadmap and security pages.
closeout: Close after release of the research or implementation.
